github.com/andy-esch/taskflow/internal/d: Audit is a code-audit document.
status: in-progress
status: fixed, landed
status: deferred, superseded, wontfix

Per-disposition finding tally (see TallyFindings), the segmented progress
bar's source. Open + Active + Done + Dropped ≤ Findings (an unrecognized or
missing status, which audit lint flags, counts toward none and falls into the
bar's empty track). OpenFindings is kept for the JSON open_findings field, the
`-c open` projection, and the "(N open)" detail suffix.
AuditBucket is an audit's lifecycle state, identical to the directory it lives in (audits/<bucket>/).
Class is a domain error's outcome category — the single, adapter-neutral classification of a sentinel-wrapped error.
Descriptor is the per-entity metadata the tool would otherwise hand-enumerate in a `switch kind` at every layer.
frontmatter a drafter fills in (not tool-managed stamps)
short, factual "how to write it" rules
top-level planning dir (TasksDir / EpicsDir / AuditsDir)
the `schema <kind>` word: task | epic | audit
the {{key}} tokens this kind's templates fill (real values at create; preview labels at show)
body scaffolds offered for this kind; the one named DefaultTemplate is used when --template is omitted
Epic is a long-lived domain goal.
FieldDoc documents one *authoring* field: the human/agent-set frontmatter a well-formed document declares.
FileProblem is a non-fatal per-file load problem: the file was skipped, with an actionable message explaining what's wrong and how to fix it.
Finding is one parsed audit finding.
XS | S | M | L
open | in-progress | fixed | landed | deferred | superseded | wontfix (see FindingStatuses)
acute | soon | eventually
FindingTally is the per-disposition finding breakdown the segmented progress bar bands by.
in-progress
fixed, landed
deferred, superseded, wontfix
open
FixResult records the auto-repairs applied (or proposed) for one file.
Issue is a single frontmatter lint finding.
NamedTemplate is one body scaffold a kind offers under a name.
Placeholder is a {{Key}} token a kind's templates may fill: the real value at create time, or Label in a placeholder preview (`template show` / `schema`).
the {{Key}} token, e.g. "title"
the preview label, e.g. "<title>"
Status is a task lifecycle state.
Task is a planning task.

Declared is the status the frontmatter literally claimed. Status is the
authoritative one (its directory); the two diverge only when a file is
misfiled — see Misfiled.
optional "snooze until" date for a deferred task (set by `task defer`)
stamped when a task enters in-progress (incl. `new --start`)
Transition is one lifecycle action: the verb a user names (from the CLI or the TUI action menu) mapped to the destination state it moves a document to.
TransitionParam marks an optional argument a lifecycle verb accepts beyond the move itself.
UnsetField is a sentinel value in a SetFields update map: the key is removed from the frontmatter instead of being assigned.
github.com/andy-esch/taskflow/internal/w: AuditJSON is the wire shape of an audit inside the --json envelopes.

The progress bar's disposition bands. open + in_progress + done + dropped ≤
findings (an unrecognized/missing status counts toward none).

ReadyToClose is true for an OPEN audit whose findings are all resolved/dropped
(none open or in-progress) — a "ready to close" call-to-action.
AuditMutationEnvelope is `audit append --json`: the reloaded audit, dry_run, and the resulting body — the audit counterpart to TaskMutationEnvelope.
AuditShowEnvelope is `audit show --json`.
AuditsEnvelope is `audit list --json`.
CountByJSON is one bucket of a finding breakdown — an urgency value or a top-level component, and its count.
CreatedEnvelope is `task/epic/audit new --json`.
CreatedItem is the created document inside CreatedEnvelope.
DoctorEnvelope is `doctor --json`: the linkback audit result.
DoctorProblem is one linkback inconsistency: the offending repo + a message.
EpicJSON is epic list output: the shared meta (embedded, so `epic list` and `epic show` can't drift) plus the task rollup.
Deprecated is the withdrawn tasks, excluded from total/done.
Liveness is the derived activity band — computed from the rollup, not stored.
Open is the pending workload (total − done); 0 = nothing in flight.
EpicMetaJSON is the shared epic meta fields, embedded by EpicJSON (`epic list`) and emitted directly by `epic show` / `epic set`.
EpicMutationEnvelope is `epic set --json`: the reloaded epic + dry_run.
EpicShowEnvelope is `epic show --json`.
EpicsEnvelope is `epic list --json`.
ErrorEnvelope is the failure payload emitted under --json (see cli.WriteError).
ErrorItem is the error body inside ErrorEnvelope.
FindingJSON is the wire shape of one audit finding.
FindingsEnvelope is `audit findings --json` (the finding-level query).
FindingsRollupJSON aggregates the actionable audit findings (status open or in-progress) across all audits — the `status` summary's "audit findings" view.
FixEnvelope is `lint --fix --json`.
InitEnvelope is `init --json`.
KindSchema is the per-kind authoring guidance (`tskflwctl schema <kind>`): how to compose a well-formed document of that kind.
LintEnvelope is `lint --json` and `audit lint --json` (the same per-entity slug+issues shape backs both).
LintTaskJSON is one entity's lint result (slug + field issues), the shape the `lint` / `audit lint` / `fix --remaining` envelopes carry per entity.
MoveResult is the per-item outcome of a transition.
MovesEnvelope is the transition report (`task start --json`, etc.).
SchemaContract is the global machine contract (`tskflwctl schema`): everything an agent needs to drive the tool without parsing --help prose.
SchemaEnvelope is `schema --json` (the global contract).
SchemaExitCode is one exit code and its stable machine name (also the `code` in the --json error envelope).
SchemaField is one known frontmatter field and its YAML storage type.
SchemaKindEnvelope is `schema <kind> --json` (per-kind authoring guidance).
SchemaStatus is one task status and whether it is part of the working set.
StatusCountJSON is one status bucket and its task count.
SummaryEnvelope is `status --json`.
TaskJSON is the wire shape of a task inside the --json envelopes.

The "<=200" cap can't be computed (struct tags are static literals) — the only
hardcoded copy of domain.MaxDescriptionLen left. Kept honest by
TestTaskJSONDescriptionTagMatchesCap; update both if the cap changes.

Misfiled/Declared surface status≠folder drift to JSON consumers (agents
are exactly who should detect it); declared_status only when misfiled.
TaskMutationEnvelope is `task set` / `task append` / `task set --body` under --json: the reloaded task, dry_run, and (for the body commands) the resulting body.
TaskShowEnvelope is `task show --json`.
TasksEnvelope is `task list --json`.
TemplateInfo is one body template's listable metadata (kind/name/description), populated by the cli for `template list`/`show`.
TemplateShowEnvelope is `template show --json` (a template's metadata + body).
TemplatesEnvelope is `template list --json`.
ThemeEntry is one color theme in `theme list --json`.
the theme this invocation resolved to
the built-in default
ThemePreviewEnvelope is `theme preview --json` — a theme's palette as data.
"dark" or "light"
ThemeSwatch is one named color in `theme preview --json`: its truecolor hex and its 16-color ANSI slot.
the 16-color slot (0..15) the CLI degrades to
the truecolor value, "#rrggbb"
the palette role (accent, red, green, …)
ThemesEnvelope is `theme list --json`.
VersionEnvelope is `version --json`.
